title: She is running Boston in honor of her mother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Madison Oakes, 23, from Boxford, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in honor of her late mother.
Her mother, diagnosed with Stage 4 brain cancer, benefited from MGB cancer center's care, living 3.5 years post-diagnosis.
Oakes is a nurse in oncology, aiming to support cancer patients and families, continuing her mother's legacy.

title: Boston police fatally shoot suspect allegedly wielding sword near Northeastern University campus
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police fatally shot a man wielding a sword near Northeastern University during a reported mental health crisis.
The suspect attacked officers and an EMS clinician, resulting in non-life-threatening injuries before police used tasers and firearms.
The incident prompted area closures and is under investigation by Boston police and the Suffolk County District Attorney's Office.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her godmother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Abigail Dubois runs the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her godmother who died of brain cancer in 2024.
Dubois supports the Cancer Care Equity program at Massachusetts General Hospital through her marathon participation.
At age 23, Dubois qualified for the Boston Marathon during the Lake Erie Marathon in September 2025.

title: 1 dead, 2 hospitalized in fatal Brockton house fire, officials say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A fatal house fire in Brockton claimed the life of a woman believed to be in her 40s.
Two residents were hospitalized following a blaze at 80 Glendale Ave, which started early Saturday.
Officials are investigating the fire's cause; initial findings suggest it started in a second-floor bedroom.

title: NH suspect who allegedly shot at family members killed in officer-involved shooting
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Masse, 38, was fatally shot by police after shooting and wounding a Nottingham officer in New Hampshire.
Masse allegedly fired at family members earlier, prompting a police search and a shelter-in-place order in Raymond.
Police found Masse hiding in the woods where an exchange of gunfire occurred; an autopsy is forthcoming.

title: She’s running Boston with her best friend after both diagnosed with cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Meg Gannon from Canton plans to run the Boston Marathon after recovering from a breast cancer diagnosis in 2024.
Gannon's best friend, Peggy, also diagnosed with cancer, will join her in the marathon to support Mass General Hospital.
Both runners aim to celebrate survivorship and inspire others with their marathon participation on April 20, 2026.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Jack Black hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Jack Black hosted 'SNL' for the fifth time on April 4, 2026, joining the show's five-timers club.
Musical guest Jack White performed for his fifth 'SNL' appearance, playing alongside Black in a music-themed sketch.
Black's episode featured cameo appearances by Jonah Hill, Tina Fey, Candice Bergen, and Melissa McCarthy.

title: Jaylen Brown opens up about past criticisms of his left hand: ‘There was some truth to it’
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jaylen Brown addressed past criticisms of his left hand, revealing struggles due to serious wrist injuries in 2021 and 2023.
Brown underwent surgery and treatments to improve his left-hand flexibility, enhancing his performance on the court.
This season, Brown averaged career-highs with 28.7 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.3 assists, elevating his MVP candidacy.

title: ‘The Red Sox were my dream job’: Don Orsillo reflects on NESN tenure, reunites with former colleagues
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Don Orsillo returns to Fenway Park with the Padres, his current team since leaving NESN in 2015.
Orsillo, former Red Sox play-by-play announcer, reflects on memorable tenure, calling it his 'dream job.'
Orsillo reunites with former NESN colleagues, sharing photos and receiving support from Red Sox fans.

title: Alex Cora explains his decision to slot Roman Anthony at DH early this season
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Roman Anthony used as designated hitter due to defensive struggles in outfield, says Red Sox manager Alex Cora.
Anthony contributed offensively with a triple and base hit, but struggled defensively against San Diego Padres.
Red Sox carry a 2-6 record into Sunday’s game against Padres at Fenway Park, aiming to improve season start.
